questions.question: What ascii gnome did Jesse Pollak claim in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary in April 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted that he claimed 'Scruffy Duskhollow' in the Gnomie Ascii Sanctuary on April 21, 2026.

questions.question: What classic video game did Brian Armstrong link to running on a Neynar-hosted app?
questions.explanation: On April 7, 2026, Brian Armstrong asked 'But can it run DOOM?' and linked to doom-snap-fc.host.neynar.app.

questions.question: What sport was Jesse Pollak celebrating with his kids in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted on March 9, 2026, about watching the cricket World Cup final with his kids.

questions.question: Which platform did Brian Armstrong use a referral code from @ace for a free month?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted a thanks to @ace for a referral to noscroll.com on April 23, 2026.

questions.question: In February 2026, Jesse's kids asked him to create virtual farms on which Farcaster channel?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted that his kids were asking for their own /fruitling-valley farms on February 15, 2026.

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak say he would wear to identify himself at /frontiertower in April 2026?
options: A base cap and blue hoodie
A whale face and an orange t-shirt
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak posted that he would be wearing 'the whale face, orange t-shirt' at the tower meetup.

questions.question: Which Pokémon-themed food did Brian Armstrong humorously tweet he would NOT buy?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong repeated three times that he would not buy SpaghettiOs just because they are Pokémon themed.

questions.question: What did Coinbase's Mircea Pasoi mint in October 2023?
questions.explanation: On October 12, 2023, the Coinbase account (via Mircea) posted that he just minted 'Farcaster Is Open For Everyone'.

questions.question: In April 2023, Mircea Pasoi built an incomplete Python port of which Hub software?
questions.explanation: The codebase port was named hub_py, which translated nodejs hubs code to Python.

questions.question: What was the price Brian Armstrong set to answer "ask me anything" via a beep in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted on March 20, 2026: 'thanks for the beep @bigeon ask me anything for $0.25'.

questions.question: Which API provider ran into unexpected downtime in June 2026 due to AWS maintenance?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak reported that maintenance downtime impacted the Neynar API, webhooks, and data products on June 15, 2026.

questions.question: In June 2026, Jesse Pollak posted "They're accepting ________ now". Fill in the blank.
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on June 26, 2026, simply states: 'They're accepting muggle money now'.

questions.question: Who did Brian Armstrong thank for the Noscroll referral code on April 23, 2026?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted: 'get 1 mo free! thanks for the referral @ace' on April 23, 2026.

questions.question: What was the exact date of the very first cast posted by the official Base account?
questions.explanation: The official Base account cast for the first time on October 24, 2022, writing: 'First cast - can't wait to see where this road takes me!'

questions.question: On March 25, 2026, Brian Armstrong asked why someone would use which messaging/communication service?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted a brief query on March 25, 2026: 'why @beeper?'

questions.question: What were the top two priorities, in order, for adding new Farcaster validators as of May 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse stated on May 27, 2026, that the top two validator priorities were geographical region (geo) and having no majority.

questions.question: Why did Jesse Pollak reject onboarding a validator running a modified fork of Snapchain?
options: It creates external verification risks and the team lacks dev capacity to verify it
questions.explanation: Jesse explained that a validator running a fork creates risk they don't have systems to verify from the outside and lack the dev capacity to take on.

questions.question: At what block height did the Snapchain mainnet shard 1 halt in April 2026?
questions.explanation: On April 11, 2026, Jesse posted that 'snapchain mainnet shard 1 has halted at ~32M height.'

questions.question: When did the Neynar acquisition deal fully close, according to Jesse's posts?
questions.explanation: Jesse's post on May 27, 2026, mentions: '...once the deal had fully closed at the beginning of april.'

questions.question: In March 2026, which token pairs faced pricing errors during wallet balance updates?
options: Tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs
questions.explanation: Jesse noted an edge case where tokens with super low liquidity USDC pairs were not priced correctly.

questions.question: What are the sequential steps in the "Neynar way" for handling bugs as shared by Jesse Pollak?
options: Linear ticket, magic, test + review, ship, reply to reporter
Discord report, patch, deploy, verify, tweet
Bug bounty, automated test, hotfix, release
questions.explanation: Jesse outlined the sequential steps: 1. Detailed linear ticket 2. magic 3. test + review 4. ship 5. reply back to the bug reporter.

questions.question: What did coding agents remind Jesse of from his grad school days at USC's GamePipe Lab?
options: Playing Dota or LoL all night
questions.explanation: Jesse recalled playing Dota or LoL all night, comparing it to the fast, fun reward loop of coding agents today.

questions.question: What latency improvement did Jesse share for @mcilroyc's backend changes in January 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse highlighted a 50% reduction in global p95 latency for Farcaster.

questions.question: According to Dan Romero in April 2026, which integration represents the most tangible mainstream use case for crypto in his 12+ years?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero posted that he was particularly excited about DoorDash as the most tangible mainstream crypto use case he's seen.

questions.question: What AI coding helper was Brian Armstrong using on "day 1" of his DoorDash project in mid-May 2026?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong posted on May 15, 2026: 'day 1 of doordash & claude code'.

questions.question: What "slop" link did Dan Romero mock on March 31, 2026?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero shared a link from @markgadala on March 31, 2026, calling it 'incredible slop'.

questions.question: Whose open source pull requests (PRs) did the Farcaster core team merge but edit significantly?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak's post on May 27, 2026, states: 'we've merged contributions that came through PRs from outside the core team, including from cassie.'

questions.question: Which country's cricket team was praised by Jesse for introducing "S tier shit posting"?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak commented on January 28, 2026: 'Cricket was missing S tier shit posting until Iceland showed up.'

questions.question: When did shard 0 of the Farcaster network cut over to the updated validator set in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak warned of instability around 12:00 PM UTC on Feb 24 when shard 0 cuts over.

questions.question: Where did Coinbase's Mircea Pasoi post a blog sharing his personal story of discovering he is on the autism spectrum?
questions.explanation: The Coinbase post linked to 'https://mirceap.com/blog/how-i-discovered-i-have-autism' on November 9, 2023.

questions.question: What did Base Official describe as having "doubled" in March 2024 without knowing why?
questions.explanation: Base Official's March 15, 2024 post states: 'last week we doubled our revenue and I have no idea why'.

questions.question: In January 2026, how did Jesse Pollak suggest making "Tom Riddle's diary" a real physical reality?
options: Using Farcaster hub data with local AI
questions.explanation: Jesse suggested combining LLMs and agents with a few more iterations of folding displays to make a physical roleplay diary.

questions.question: During Farcaster's backend database layer update on February 5, 2026, what time window had potential web/mobile degradation?
questions.explanation: Jesse specified database layer changes would occur 'between noon - 1PM PST' on February 5, 2026.

questions.question: Which humanoid robot model did Jesse Pollak mention as a prime candidate for an OpenClaw agent to control?
questions.explanation: On February 3, 2026, Jesse posted: 'Has someone already given an OpenClaw agent access to a humanoid like the G1 to move around their home?'

questions.question: What was the primary language used by Mircea Pasoi to build the incomplete hub port in April 2023?
questions.explanation: Mircea Pasoi built a 'Python port of @farcaster/hub-nodejs' called hub_py.

questions.question: What leaderboard did Dan Romero joke he had "really fallen off" on in May 2026?
questions.explanation: Dan Romero wrote: 'i’ve really fallen of if @v is higher on a poasting leaderboard'.

questions.question: What was the custom game link shared by Brian Armstrong to play Pokémon together on the same screen?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong linked to https://pokemon-snap.host.neynar.app/game in his post on April 9, 2026.

questions.question: In November 2022, Base Official shared an article comparing crypto journalism to early coverage of what historical era?
questions.explanation: Base Official shared a Quorum Media piece comparing early internet journalism to crypto reporting today.

questions.question: In May 2026, where did Jesse Pollak test moving one of their testnet nodes to pave the way for validator decentralization?
questions.explanation: Jesse's post on May 1, 2026 stated: 'this week - we're testing moving one our testnet nodes to a different continent.'

questions.question: In Brian Armstrong's post on April 2, 2026, what device did the ASCII skeleton hold?
questions.explanation: The ASCII art shows a skeleton holding a pager/beeper (represented by the 📟 emoji).

questions.question: What simple criteria did Jesse Pollak outline for adding new Farcaster validators in May 2026?
options: Run snapchain, not be neynar, and be in a different geographical region
Run a fork of snapchain, be a community member, and have high uptime
Hold 100k native tokens, run hub-nodejs, and be in Europe
Run snapchain, be audited by Neynar, and utilize AWS
questions.explanation: Jesse stated: 'our criteria for adding validators right now is simple: run snapchain, not be neynar and be in a geographical region different from the current validator set.'

questions.question: What did Jesse Pollak post on April 2, 2026, foreshadowing major Farcaster shard events?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted 'oh snap' on April 2, 2026.

questions.question: Why does Jesse Pollak advocate for "decentralizing one variable at a time" on Farcaster?
options: To prevent Sybil attacks from Neynar users
To slowly phase out AWS dependency
To prioritize network stability and growth first
questions.explanation: Jesse argued that stability and growth of the network must come first: 'a decentralized protocol that nobody uses doesn't serve anyone.'

questions.question: What is the URL of the newsletter where the writer discusses things "no one tells you how to think about," as shared by Base Official in March 2024?
questions.explanation: Base Official linked to ashprabaker.com/p/i-just-wanna-go-viral-on-tiktok-help.

questions.question: Who wrote the article about discovering they were on the autism spectrum, which was shared on the Coinbase handle in late 2023?
questions.explanation: The Coinbase post linked to mirceap.com, the blog of Mircea Pasoi.

questions.question: What is the exact title of the FIP (Farcaster Improvement Proposal) Jesse Pollak requested feedback on on April 23, 2026?
questions.explanation: Jesse's post was: 'Looking for feedback or reviews on the Snapchain Signers FIP'.

questions.question: In March 2026, which Farcaster user sent a beep to Brian Armstrong that prompted an AMA?
questions.explanation: Brian Armstrong's post on March 20, 2026: 'thanks for the beep @bigeon'.

questions.question: Which Neynar engineer was praised by Jesse Pollak in February 2026 for a highly-structured bug-to-resolution pipeline?
questions.explanation: Jesse Pollak highlighted: '@grin is on a mission to send bugs right to jail!'

questions.question: What was the YouTube video ID shared by Jesse Pollak on April 12, 2026, regarding AI geography?
questions.explanation: The link shared is https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aUM4kv0HnG0.

questions.question: How long after the Snapchain shard 1 halt on April 11, 2026, did Jesse Pollak confirm the network was stable and producing blocks again?
questions.explanation: Jesse posted both the halt notice and the resolution notice on the exact same day, April 11, 2026.
